mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as portfolio rebalancing flows into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of May 2009 indicates emergency accommodation
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate stabilization or decline signals labor market recovery and renewed consumer confidence. When UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window, equity investors anticipate sustained earnings growth from increased consumer spending, driving positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.4% in May 2009 remains elevated but potentially stabilizing from crisis peak
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE declines > 0.2pp are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) recovery from crisis lows signals renewed construction activity, employment multipliers, and consumer wealth effects. When HOUST rises above 450 thousand units annualized, forward equity returns accelerate over 20 days as investors price in sustained demand-side momentum and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 458.0 thousand units in April 2009 signals early recovery
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when HOUST > 450 are not significantly positive (mean < 0.3%, t-test p > 0.15), or signal fails to outperform baseline by at least 2% annualized Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates when supply-side capacity utilization rises, signaling pricing power and margin expansion for equities. When INDPRO rises > 1.5% over a 3-month window, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days turn positive as investors anticipate earnings surprises from operational leverage.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.07 in April 2009 shows early recovery from crisis trough
regime_hint: supply-side recovery and capacity utilization expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO growth > 1.5% are not significantly positive (mean < 0.5%), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS grows > 1% month-over-month AND HOUST rises above 450 units, the combined demand signal produces positive 20-day equity returns as investors recognize synchronized broad-based recovery. The interaction captures non-linear amplification: housing recovery without retail growth is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 1% AND HOUST > 450 thousand units simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 339,993M and HOUST at 458K in April-May 2009 show coordinated demand recovery
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when both conditions trigger are not significantly higher than when only one triggers (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10), or joint signal produces positive returns in < 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market tightening and wage pressure. When PAYEMS grows > 0.3% month-over-month AND UNRATE declines simultaneously, equity investors anticipate margin compression but also sustained consumer demand. The net effect on 5-day returns depends on the relative magnitude: strong payroll growth dominates, producing positive returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth > 0.3% AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132,151K and UNRATE at 9.4% in May 2009 show early labor stabilization
regime_hint: labor market stabilization with wage pressure emergence
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when both conditions trigger are not significantly positive (mean < 0.2%), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued (year-over-year growth < 2%) while monetary policy is accommodative (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%). This regime allows the Fed to maintain emergency support without inflation concerns, sustaining equity valuations. When both conditions hold, 60-day forward returns are positive as investors expect policy continuity and real rate compression.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2% AND FEDFUNDS < 0.5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.67 and FEDFUNDS at 0.18% in April-May 2009 show disinflationary environment with emergency accommodation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both conditions hold are not significantly positive (mean < 0.8%), or the interaction coefficient between low inflation and low rates is not significant (p > 0.12) in explaining forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal demand destruction and recession risk. When INDPRO falls > 1% month-over-month, equity investors anticipate earnings downgrades and increased default risk. This produces negative 20-day forward returns as portfolio managers reduce risk exposure and rotate to defensive assets.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.07 in April 2009 shows recovery trajectory, but mechanism applies if trajectory reverses
regime_hint: demand contraction and recession risk regime
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 1% MoM are not significantly negative (mean > -0.2%), or signal produces negative returns in < 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
